logs:
[7:10:32 pm] POST /sendFeedback HTTP/1.1
    host: altoro.testfire.net
    connection: keep-alive
    user-agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; rv:45.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/45.0
    accept-language: en-US
    accept-encoding: gzip, deflate, br
    accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
    content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
    referer: http://altoro.testfire.net/feedback.jsp
    cookie: JSESSIONID=4CD71DA2BE1AFEEED4BA15579F97082C
    content-length: 144
    Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

    cfile=comments.txt&name=default&email_addr=%3CSvG%0AoNloAd%3Dalert%28%27w1cmeduv9d%27%29%3E&subject=default&submit=%20Submit%20&comments=Hi%20there%21
[7:11:05 pm] A report has been generated in the file C:\Users\yogi\AppData\Local\Temp\VAPT Engine-Zp6PnS\report.json

findings.title: Cleartext Password Submission
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to Cleartext Submission of Password. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: GET parameter 'passw' is vulnerable. Password field passw sent in clear text from URL http://altoro.testfire.net/login.jsp
findings.evidence: GET parameter 'passw' is vulnerable. Password field passw sent in clear text from URL http://altoro.testfire.net/login.jsp
findings.remediation: Ensure all login forms submit over HTTPS. Redirect HTTP to HTTPS automatically.
findings.impact: Medium Impact: Passwords submitted over HTTP can be intercepted by network attackers.
findings.stepsToReproduce:
1. **Target**: The parameter 'passw' was identified as vulnerable.
2. **Action**: Use the provided Reproduction URL or Curl Command to replicate the request exactly.
3. **Verification**: Analyze the response for the specific behavior described in the 'Impact' section.
findings.payload: Standard testing payload (see steps to reproduce)

findings.title: Missing CSP Header
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to Content Security Policy Configuration. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: CSP is not set for URL: http://altoro.testfire.net/
findings.evidence: CSP is not set for URL: http://altoro.testfire.net/
findings.remediation: Implement a strict Content-Security-Policy header to prevent XSS, clickjacking, and data injection attacks.
findings.impact: Info: Missing CSP header allows injection of unauthorized scripts, frames, and resources.
findings.stepsToReproduce:
1. **Target**: The parameter '' was identified as vulnerable.
2. **Action**: Use the provided Reproduction URL or Curl Command to replicate the request exactly.
3. **Verification**: Analyze the response for the specific behavior described in the 'Impact' section.
findings.payload: Standard testing payload (see steps to reproduce)

findings.title: Missing X-Frame-Options
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to Clickjacking Protection. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: X-Frame-Options is not set
findings.evidence: X-Frame-Options is not set
findings.remediation: Add X-Frame-Options: DENY or SAMEORIGIN header. Use CSP frame-ancestors directive.
findings.impact: Info: Without X-Frame-Options, the page can be embedded in malicious iframes for clickjacking attacks.
findings.stepsToReproduce:
1. **Target**: The parameter '' was identified as vulnerable.
2. **Action**: Use the provided Reproduction URL or Curl Command to replicate the request exactly.
3. **Verification**: Analyze the response for the specific behavior described in the 'Impact' section.
findings.payload: Standard testing payload (see steps to reproduce)

findings.title: Missing X-Content-Type-Options
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to MIME Type Confusion. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: X-Content-Type-Options is not set
findings.evidence: X-Content-Type-Options is not set
findings.remediation: Add X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header to prevent MIME sniffing attacks.
findings.impact: Info: Without X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, browsers may misinterpret file types.
findings.stepsToReproduce:
1. **Target**: The parameter '' was identified as vulnerable.
2. **Action**: Use the provided Reproduction URL or Curl Command to replicate the request exactly.
3. **Verification**: Analyze the response for the specific behavior described in the 'Impact' section.
findings.payload: Standard testing payload (see steps to reproduce)

findings.title: Unencrypted Communication
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to Unencrypted Channels. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: Sensitive data (cookie in the response) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://altoro.testfire.net/. The server did not enforce HTTPS.
findings.evidence: Sensitive data (cookie in the response) was sent over an unencrypted HTTP connection to http://altoro.testfire.net/. The server did not enforce HTTPS.
findings.remediation: Review and remediate the identified security issue. Follow OWASP security guidelines and industry best practices.

findings.title: Missing Secure Flag (Cookie)
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to Secure Flag cookie. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: Secure flag is not set on the cookie: 'JSESSIONID' set at 'http://altoro.testfire.net/'
findings.evidence: Secure flag is not set on the cookie: 'JSESSIONID' set at 'http://altoro.testfire.net/'
findings.remediation: Set Secure flag on all cookies transmitted over HTTPS to prevent interception via Man-in-the-Middle.
findings.impact: Info: Cookies transmitted without Secure flag can be intercepted over unencrypted connections.
findings.stepsToReproduce:
1. **Target**: The parameter '' was identified as vulnerable.
2. **Action**: Use the provided Reproduction URL or Curl Command to replicate the request exactly.
3. **Verification**: Analyze the response for the specific behavior described in the 'Impact' section.

2. **Action**: Use the provided Reproduction URL or Curl Command to replicate the request exactly.
3. **Verification**: Analyze the response for the specific behavior described in the 'Impact' section.

findings.title: Server Error (500)
findings.description: The application appears to be vulnerable to Internal Server Error. This issue could identify a security weakness that might be exploited to compromise the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of the application.
findings.summary: GET parameter 'content' is vulnerable. The server responded with a 500 HTTP error code while attempting to inject a payload in the parameter content
findings.evidence: GET parameter 'content' is vulnerable. The server responded with a 500 HTTP error code while attempting to inject a payload in the parameter content
findings.remediation: Review and remediate the identified security issue. Follow OWASP security guidelines and industry best practices.
findings.impact: Info: Server errors may reveal stack traces, internal paths, or configuration details.
